meta.subject: Commerce
meta.philosophy: The Commerce syllabus emphasises on providing learners with basic knowledge, skills and positive values as participants in the world of business, economics and finance. It covers a wide range of topics including Home and International Trade, Entrepreneurship and Personal Financial Management.
meta.competence_learning: The Competence Based Curriculum utilises a learner-centred approach, which entails that the teaching and learning process is learner-centred in nature. The emphasis is on what learners are expected to do rather than what they are expected to know, ensuring that learners develop competences that can contribute to the development of society.
goals: Demonstrate knowledge of conducting business internationally
Use suitable methods of buying and selling on credit
Demonstrate ways of protecting consumers from exploitative tendencies
Apply knowledge of personal financial management

specific_outcomes: 2.1.4.1 Apply safety measures to prevent fraudulent activities/situations
2.1.5.1 Show knowledge of calculating Wages and Salaries
2.1.6.1 Apply knowledge of personal financial management
2.1.6.3 Engage in different methods of Investment

subtopics: 2.2.1 Methods of buying and selling on credit
specific_outcomes: 2.2.1.1 Use suitable methods of buying and selling on credit
2.3.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of conducting business internationally
2.6.1.1 Demonstrate ways of protecting consumers from exploitative tendencies
